steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: This is a Rhetorical Synthesis question. The student wants to place the 1821 Declaration of Independence in the context of de Leon's changing political beliefs. The correct choice must connect three elements from the notes: (1) de Leon had long opposed independence, (2) Aranda's 1820 revolt changed his mind, and (3) de Leon wrote the 1821 Declaration. All three elements must be present and accurate.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — Colonel Aranda's revolt was the inspiration that de Leon, a long-standing opponent of Central American independence, needed to change his political beliefs.: Mentions the revolt and de Leon's prior opposition, but does not connect the changed beliefs to the writing of the 1821 Declaration. The goal is to place the Declaration in context, which this choice fails to do. ✗
  • Choice B — Long an opponent of Central American independence, de Leon changed his mind after an 1820 revolt and wrote the 1821 declaration.: Captures all three required elements: de Leon's prior opposition, the 1820 revolt as the turning point, and his writing of the 1821 Declaration. This places the Declaration directly in the context of his changing beliefs. ✓
  • Choice C — A change in de Leon's political beliefs that occurred when the Spanish king repealed the 1812 constitution led to de Leon writing Central America's Declaration of Independence.: Gets the cause wrong — according to the notes, de Leon changed his mind after Aranda's 1820 revolt, not when the king repealed the 1812 constitution (which happened in 1814, years earlier). ✗
  • Choice D — The writing of Central America's Declaration of Independence may not have happened were it not for Colonel Aranda's 1820 revolt.: Uses speculative language ("may not have happened") and omits de Leon's changing political beliefs entirely, which are central to the stated goal. ✗
That matches Choice B — Long an opponent of Central American independence, de Leon changed his mind after an 1820 revolt and wrote the 1821 declaration..
